highlight.title: Historic On-Screen Assessment Consultation Now Open
highlight.summary: <cite index='31-2,31-3'>Ofqual is launching a 12-week public consultation on its proposed approach to regulating on-screen exams in GCSEs, AS and A levels, with proposals aiming to support responsible innovation while protecting fairness, standards and the integrity of England's qualifications system.</cite> <cite index='31-1'>The consultation will run until 5 March 2026, and responses will inform Ofqual's final approach to regulating on-screen assessments.</cite>

resources.title: KS2 SATs 2026: What Parents Need to Know
resources.why_now: Parents need to know <cite index='21-2'>this year's KS2 assessments take place over four days from 11 to 14 May 2026</cite> and <cite index='21-12'>the government has provided schools with new resources and support, including a Writing Framework for teachers, reading support through English Hubs, and preparation materials through Oak National Academy.</cite>

resources.title: Oak National Academy Free Lessons (KS1-KS4)
resources.why_now: Students can access <cite index='13-1,13-2'>free online lessons from KS1 to KS4, with videos, quizzes and lessons organised by year group and subject</cite> - perfect for independent revision across all secondary year groups.

resources.title: GCSE & A-Level Formula Support Extended to 2027
resources.why_now: Students taking <cite index='9-15'>GCSE mathematics, physics and combined science exams in 2025, 2026 and 2027</cite> will receive formula sheets, providing crucial support for current Year 9-11 students and their parents planning revision strategies.

resources.title: Ofqual Consultation: Shape Digital Exam Future
resources.why_now: Parents and teachers can influence <cite index='31-9,31-10'>how up to 2 new on-screen specifications from each exam board will be introduced, though the most popular subjects — those with over 100,000 entries nationally — would not be permitted as on-screen exams at this stage.</cite>

claude_take: The education system is at a pivotal moment with digital transformation beginning but proceeding cautiously - the Ofqual consultation represents the biggest change to exam delivery in decades. Meanwhile, government support for struggling students continues with extended formula sheets and enhanced SATs preparation resources, showing commitment to post-pandemic recovery.
